campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met on September 2, 2025, to review how the city's money was managed in July, including investments and general finances. The council also discussed plans to improve transportation options around Campbell, looking at ways to make getting around the city easier for residents.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment and cash transactions review
City treasurer's financial report for July
Campbell Multimodal Transportation Plan (improving transit, biking, walking options)
Transportation workplan development and next steps

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing to get community input on a new rule or regulation. After hearing from residents, the council introduced an ordinance (new city rule) and decided to skip the usual first reading step to speed up the process. The ordinance is scheduled to be formally approved at the next council meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held to gather resident input on proposed ordinance
First reading of ordinance waived to expedite approval process
Ordinance scheduled for final adoption at next regular council meeting
Specific ordinance topic not detailed in agenda summary

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from multiple sessions held in March and to authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda was primarily administrative, focused on documenting previous meetings and moving forward with a construction project.
keyTopics: Approved minutes from six separate March meetings
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Multiple closed session discussions (details not disclosed)
Joint study sessions between council members

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 8, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to plan upcoming meetings scheduled for April 14 and April 21, 2026. They reviewed what items should be added to these agendas, set time limits for specific discussions, and checked whether proper public notices were given for any agenda items or documents.
keyTopics: Planning the April 14, 2026 City Council meeting agenda
Planning the April 21, 2026 City Council meeting agenda
Setting time limits for specific agenda items
Reviewing public notice requirements for agenda items
Public records from March 26 - April 2, 2026

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 that allowed residents to participate either in person or online. Residents who wanted to speak during public comment could join via Zoom or phone and use the raise hand feature to be called on by the council chair.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom and phone dial-in
Public comment procedures for residents
Hybrid in-person and online meeting format

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 7, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held their regular meeting on April 7, 2026, where they opened with the Pledge of Allegiance and dedicated time to recognize Arab-American Heritage Month and Fair Housing Month. The meeting highlighted these two important observances to celebrate the city's diverse communities and promote fair housing practices for all residents.
keyTopics: Arab-American Heritage Month recognition
Fair Housing Month observance
Community diversity celebration
Housing discrimination awareness

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss several community initiatives including a food and budget program, a youth-led organization called Unmuted Tabs, and summer job opportunities for young people in the city. The council also approved meeting minutes from their previous session and opened the floor for residents to speak about local issues.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth-led organization
Summer job opportunities for Cupertino youth
Public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, using a hybrid format that allowed people to attend in person or join remotely via Zoom. Residents can participate by watching online, calling in by phone, or submitting written comments through the city's website before the meeting.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom attendance available)
Remote participation options via Zoom video conference
Phone dial-in access for residents without internet
Written public comment submissions through city website

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review their spending plans and budget priorities for 2025-2026, including updates to a federal funding plan. They also discussed changing rules about how council vacancies are filled and approved meeting minutes from previous sessions.
keyTopics: Updates to the city's 5-year community development and housing plan
Changes to how the city fills empty council seats
Council members' requests for new projects and work items
Approval of previous meeting minutes and scheduling of future meetings

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council met to approve the city's gas utility budget and rates for the next fiscal year. The main action involved reviewing financial plans for the gas utility, moving money between accounts, and adjusting how much residents and businesses will pay for natural gas service based on updated cost forecasts.
keyTopics: Gas utility rates going up for residential customers (G-1 schedule)
Rate changes for apartment buildings and commercial properties (G-2 schedule)
Rate adjustments for large commercial gas users (G-3 schedule)
Budget reserve transfers and general fund adjustments for gas utility
Fiscal Year 2027 gas utility financial forecast approval
